title: opicrina latifolia
intro: Opicrina latifolia, also known as the Mexican tea tree or white guava, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Opicrina latifolia include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make topical creams and ointments, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
